task_summary: Construct the managed hotplug implemetation and updating the schematic and layout for review.
worklogs:
── 2026-03-12 ──
- Discussion with Portwell and Apps for the managed hotplug implementation.
- Sketch the control flow chart to Portwell.
    [IMG_B64:]

── 2026-03-17 ──
- Managed Hotplug discussion
  - Teams meeting with Portwell team to clarify the managed hotplug design and control flow.
  - The IO expander needs to connect to BMC because Portwell needs to do MCIO lifetime counting.
  - The attention button on Host board, when the user presses the button then BMC will release the HP_IN to MAX5954 → HP_OUT (Attention Button)->PM60160-> MAIN_SW1_HP_OUT_R (Power Controller)
    [IMG_B64:]

- Javelin SI simulation
  - The result is positive if w/o TVS component is included. Portwell also confirmed that the SI result is 100% failed if TVS component is included.
    [IMG_B64:]
  - The simulation without TVS but keeps the stubs there, the SI result is PASS. It represents that if the TVS is not installed then the PCIe lane should not be impacted.
  - According to PRD, the TVS component will only be installed on 2 of 4 DSPs for validation further. If the validation still fail, then Portwell will remove the TVS future.
- Schedule updated
  - Gerber release: 4/27
  - PCBA build: 5/26
  - Bring-up: 5/29
  - Demonstrate to NVIDIA: TBD

── 2026-03-24 ──
- Release Beta RC1 firmware to Portwell for FVB64 validation
- Portwell will start the Javelin FVB with CX8 validation in house, 3/25~3/26.
- Portwell reported some gating issue (link speed downgrade to Gen1) when they enable the hotplug function at host side.
  - The issue does happen when booting to BIOS (no enter to OS).

── 2026-03-27 ──
- Per Portwell cross checked, they find this issue caused by the BIOS and some unknow issues on the host. After relashed the BIOS and change the host, the link speed issue has resolved.
- Portwell has started the CX8 stress test today. The test result would be available on next week.
- Once CX8 tested with no issue, they will arragne the validation with PG-550 at CM next two weeks.

task_summary: FXN is working on PCI1004 design for Pure Storage 2U LE-FA project
worklogs:
── 2026-01-08 ──
- Inquiry from Foxconn Devin Huang
  *We are surveying a PCIe Gen.4 switch with 2 x8 upstream and 2 x8 NTB downstream for an 2U LE storage server project. Not sure if Trident series switch chip with 28 lanes or 36 lanes for our project is recommended by Microchip.*

── 2026-01-13 ──
[IMG_B64:]
[IMG_B64:]

── 2026-02-05 ──
- FXN Devin confirm that the project updated
  1. End Customer:Pure Storage
  2. Project name: Granite/Topaz
  3. Application: Storage System
  4. Platform: AMD SP6 (EPYC 8004) Storage Platform
  5. OEM/ODM: ODM
  6. Sample schedule: TBD (per project NPI schedule)
  7. MP schedule: TBD
  8. Forecast(per month):TBD
- Granite is a 2U storage rack.

── 2026-03-13 ──
- Updated from FXN
  *Our customer has changed PCIe switch solution to Switchtec Gen4 16-lane solution PCI1004 to support NTB x8.*

── 2026-03-19 ──
- Visit Devin Huang for application discussion because PM40036 change to PCI1004
  - The PCI1004 is used only as a two-node bridge with NTB.
  - Pure Storage and Devin are not aware that the PCI1004 only supports up to x4 bifurcation.
  - Devin will confirm the PCI1004 bifurcation limitation with Pure Storage.
  - Request Devin to update the block diagram to accommodate the PCI1004 solution.


── 2026-03-23 ──
- Devin asked the alternative SPI flash because SST26WF064C-104I/SM is not in their CIS system. Also they are not willing to use automotive grade SPI flash, so they request to commit non-qualification SPI flash (industrial temp grade).
- Since Lanner's experience that non-qualified SPI can cause the the firmware load failed. So I am strongly recommend using SST26WF064C as primary source.
- Keynes will submit L2 ticket to NCS for new SPI flash qualification possibility.


── 2026-03-25 ──
- Per Devin, Pure Storage considering to have two PCI1004 to balance the bandwidth concern of the NTB connection. So far sounds like Pure Storage is accepted the x4 bifurcation.
- Devin will update the block diagram once they firm from Pure Storage.

── 2026-03-06 ──
- Question from Foxconn Diag engineer about the loopback test
Hi Keynes,
 
I did 2 loopback tests (shown below) based on the following topology:
[IMG_B64:]

Based on the test result, I have 2 questions:
1. Can we say that the loopback mode is set OK if the injected errors are detected and reported correctly?
2. For test 2, the injected errors are still detected and reported even if the PM40068 DownStream is not in "Loop Back (ACTIVE)" mode, 
     is "Far-End" loopback supported for this topology (SW DownStream <---> SW UpStream)?
 
Please help check these questions, thank you!
 
test 1.
  set PM40068 DownStream to TX->RX and LTSSM loopback:
  the port status is changed to "Loop Back (ACTIVE)", and the PCIe speed/width changed to 16GT/s, x0,
      [IMG_B64:] 
      
 when generating pattern and inject errors, the injected errors are detected.
      [IMG_B64:] 
 
 
test 2.
  set PM40068 DownStream to LTSSM loopback, and set PM40036 UpStream to RX-TX loopback mode:
  PM40068 DownStream port changed between "Detect (...)" and "Loop Back (...)" repeatedly,
       [IMG_B64:]
 
  when generating pattern, PM40068 DownStream became "Detect (ACTIVE0)", the injected errors are still detected even if the PM40068 DownStream is not "Loop Back (ACTIVE)"
       [IMG_B64:]

worklogs:
── 2026-03-26 ──
- FXN request the JIRA support for loopback test on PM50052
*Does the SERDES support External (Analog) Loopback where data travels through the physical package/pins?*
*We need to confirm if the loopback data path is:*
*1. Internal (Digital): Data stays within the PCS/PMA layer.*
*2. External (Analog/Physical): Data travels through the Tx/Rx pins and requires an external cable/connection.*
*Since we are testing the Switch Card standalone, we need to know if we can achieve Link-up and data validation by simply cross-connecting two ports. *

task_summary: Dell Update Package (DUP) for PCIe SW
worklogs:
── 2026-03-24 ──
- Inquiry from Dell Taiwan Tony Huang

The background is to support Dell Update Package for PCIe SW.
Dell Update Package (DUP) is a self-contained executable, encapsulating the device and dependency in a consistent easy-to-execute user interface, for updating software component on the system.
[redistribution] Do we have permission to ship the MCHP tool inside a DUP package?
[automation] Does the tool support silent/non-interactive execution?
[inventory] How do we programmatically detect both PCIe switch chips and read current FW versions?
[applicability] What IDs and version rules determine whether firmware applies?
[reboot] Is reboot or AC power cycle required after update?
[multi-chip sequencing] If there are 2 switch chips, must they be updated in a specific order?
[error recovery] What is the recovery method if update fails midway?
[signing] Is the firmware payload already vendor-signed, and does DUP need additional package signing only?
[support matrix] Which OSes are supported for in-band flashing?


── 2026-03-25 ──
[redistribution] Do we have permission to ship the MCHP tool inside a DUP package ?
[[KH]] ChipLink is not allowed to ship to any unauthorized users. The alternative is management utility called switchtec-kernel and switchtec-user. These are open source on Microsemi GitHub. Please read the detail in Microchip PCIe switch spec, the Switch Management section. 
https://github.com/Microsemi/switchtec-kernel
https://github.com/Microsemi/switchtec-user

[automation] Does the tool support silent/non-interactive execution?[[KH]]  Please read the device spec for more detail.
[inventory] How do we programmatically detect both PCIe switch chips and read current FW versions?[[KH]]  Thru switchtec-user CLI, you can use the sub command “fw-info” to get firmware information, and “info” to get HW rev, DID..etc.
[applicability] What IDs and version rules determine whether firmware applies? [[KH]] There is no positive correlation. 
[reboot] Is reboot or AC power cycle required after update?[[KH]]  Yes, the PCIe switch to be reset to load the new firmware and configuration. 
[multi-chip sequencing] If there are 2 switch chips, must they be updated in a specific order?[[KH]] Not required.
[error recovery] What is the recovery method if update fails midway?[[KH]]  The interface can be TWI, UART, and JTAG. Please refer to device spec, APPENDIX B: Switch Disaster Recovery
[signing] Is the firmware payload already vendor-signed, and does DUP need additional package signing only?[[KH]]  As I recall, Bombarde uses unsigned firmware. If you wish to use signed firmware (which we call Secure Boot), it will be a completely different matter. I would recommend checking Section 9 (Switch Security) in the device specification for more details. Please note that once the Secure Boot feature is enabled, debugging and RMA processes will become significantly more complicated.
[support matrix] Which OSes are supported for in-band flashing?[[KH]]  We recommend uses Linux for in-band flashing. We do have Windows signed driver (only for Windows Server OS) but not easy use for switch management.
